revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''吉志舞'''（きしまい）とは[[大嘗祭]]などで、[[安倍氏]]の当主等が監督して、[[闕腋袍]]（けってきのほう）等、主に[[...'
revision.text:
'''吉志舞'''（きしまい）とは[[大嘗祭]]などで、[[安倍氏]]の当主等が監督して、[[闕腋袍]]（けってきのほう）等、主に[[武官]]の服装で、踊られた[[舞楽]]。 '''吉師舞'''、'''吉士舞'''等とも表記される。舞い方自体は伝承されていない。[[楯節舞]]（たてふしまい：楯伏舞、楯臥舞等とも表記）と同じものと考えられる。 伝説では、[[神功皇后]]が[[新羅征伐]]後、凱旋し、安倍氏の祖先によって、大嘗祭で踊られたものであるという。 また、[[住吉大社]]には、神功皇后帰還の時、出迎えた、現在[[堺市]]にある[[七道駅|七道]]浜の住人が、傘を被って踊ったものが起源であり、[[住吉踊り]]はこの伝統を継承している、との伝承がある。
[[吉師]]とは[[大和朝廷]]にて外交や水軍などに関係した氏族を指し、渡来系のものが多かったという。安倍氏は吉師と関係があった。
[[帝国海軍]]の依頼で、[[伊福部昭]]により作曲された同名の古典風軍樂と題された曲がある。戦後、[[ゴジラ]]等の映画で[[自衛隊]]の出動する場面などに背景音楽としてよく使われた。

'''吉志舞'''（きしまい）とは[[大嘗祭]]などで、[[安倍氏]]の当主等が監督して、[[闕腋袍]]（けってきのほう）等、主に[[武官]]の服装で、踊られた[[舞楽]]。 '''吉師舞'''、'''吉士舞'''等とも表記される。舞い方自体は伝承されていない。[[楯節舞]]（たてふしまい：楯伏舞、楯臥舞等とも表記）と同じものと考えられる。 伝説では、[[神功皇后]]が[[新羅征伐]]後、凱旋し、安倍氏の祖先によって、大嘗祭で踊られたものであるという。 また、[[住吉大社]]には、神功皇后帰還の時、出迎えた、現在[[堺市]]にある[[七道駅|七道]]浜の住人が、傘を被って踊ったものが起源であり、[[住吉踊り]]はこの伝統を継承している、との伝承がある。
[[吉師]]とは[[大和朝廷]]にて外交や水軍などに関係した氏族を指し、渡来系のものが多かったという。安倍氏は吉師と関係があった。
[[帝国海軍]]の依頼で、[[伊福部昭]]により作曲された同名の古典風軍樂と題された曲がある。戦後、[[ゴジラ]]等の映画で[[自衛隊]]の出動する場面などに背景音楽としてよく使われた。
[[Category:日本舞踊|きしまい]] [[Category:日本の伝統音楽|きしまい]]

'''吉志舞'''（きしまい）とは[[大嘗祭]]などで、[[安倍氏]]の当主等が監督して、[[闕腋袍]]（けってきのほう）等、主に[[武官]]の服装で、踊られた[[舞楽]]。 '''吉師舞'''、'''吉士舞'''等とも表記される。舞い方自体は伝承されていない。[[楯節舞]]（たてふしまい：楯伏舞、楯臥舞等とも表記）と同じものと考えられる。 伝説では、[[神功皇后]]が[[新羅征伐]]後、凱旋し、安倍氏の祖先によって、大嘗祭で踊られたものであるという。 また、[[住吉大社]]には、神功皇后帰還の時、出迎えた、現在[[堺市]]にある[[七道駅|七道]]浜の住人が、傘を被って踊ったものが起源であり、[[住吉踊り]]はこの伝統を継承している、との伝承がある。
[[吉師]]とは[[大和朝廷]]にて外交や水軍などに関係した氏族を指し、渡来系のものが多かったという。安倍氏は吉師と関係があった。
[[大日本帝国海軍|帝国海軍]]の依頼で、[[伊福部昭]]により作曲された同名の古典風軍樂と題された曲がある。戦後、[[ゴジラ]]等の映画で[[自衛隊]]の出動する場面などに背景音楽としてよく使われた。
{{DEFAULTSORT:きしまい}} [[Category:日本舞踊]] [[Category:日本の伝統音楽]]

'''吉志舞'''（きしまい）とは[[大嘗祭]]などで、[[安倍氏]]の当主等が監督して、[[闕腋袍]]（けってきのほう）等、主に[[武官]]の服装で、踊られた[[舞楽]]。 '''吉師舞'''、'''吉士舞'''等とも表記される。舞い方自体は伝承されていない。[[楯節舞]]（たてふしまい：楯伏舞、楯臥舞等とも表記）と同じものと考えられる。 伝説では、[[神功皇后]]が[[新羅征伐]]後、凱旋し、安倍氏の祖先によって、大嘗祭で踊られたものであるという。 また、[[住吉大社]]には、神功皇后帰還の時、出迎えた、現在[[堺市]]にある[[七道駅|七道]]浜の住人が、傘を被って踊ったものが起源であり、[[住吉踊り]]はこの伝統を継承している、との伝承がある。
[[吉師]]とは[[大和朝廷]]にて外交や水軍などに関係した氏族を指し、渡来系のものが多かったという。安倍氏は吉師と関係があった。
[[大日本帝国海軍|帝国海軍]]の依頼で、[[伊福部昭]]により作曲された同名の古典風軍樂と題された曲がある。戦後、[[ゴジラ]]等の映画で[[自衛隊]]の出動する場面などに背景音楽としてよく使われた。そのため、「怪獣大戦争マーチ」と呼ばれている。
{{DEFAULTSORT:きしまい}} [[Category:日本舞踊]] [[Category:日本の伝統音楽]]

'''吉志舞'''（きしまい）とは[[大嘗祭]]などで、[[安倍氏]]の当主等が監督して、[[闕腋袍]]（けってきのほう）等、主に[[武官]]の服装で、踊られた[[舞楽]]。 '''吉師舞'''、'''吉士舞'''等とも表記される。舞い方自体は伝承されていない。[[楯節舞]]（たてふしまい：楯伏舞、楯臥舞等とも表記）と同じものと考えられる。 伝説では、[[神功皇后]]が[[新羅征伐]]後、凱旋し、安倍氏の祖先によって、大嘗祭で踊られたものであるという。 また、[[住吉大社]]には、神功皇后帰還の時、出迎えた、現在[[堺市]]にある[[七道駅|七道]]浜の住人が、傘を被って踊ったものが起源であり、[[住吉踊り]]はこの伝統を継承している、との伝承がある。
[[吉師]]とは[[大和朝廷]]にて外交や水軍などに関係した氏族を指し、渡来系のものが多かったという。安倍氏は吉師と関係があった。
[[大日本帝国海軍|帝国海軍]]の依頼で、[[伊福部昭]]により作曲された同名の古典風軍樂と題された曲がある。戦後、[[ゴジラ]]等の映画で[[自衛隊]]の出動する場面などに背景音楽としてよく使われた。『[[ゴジラ_(1954年の映画)|ゴジラ]]』の「フリゲートマーチ」（[[海上保安庁]]の[[巡視船]]や[[アメリカ海軍]]の[[フリゲート]]艦隊のテーマ）、『[[宇宙大戦争]]』の「宇宙大戦争マーチ」（映画のメインテーマ）などといった異なる編曲が使用されたが、なかでも『[[怪獣大戦争]]』での編曲（映画のメインテーマ）が「怪獣大戦争マーチ」として知られるようになった。「怪獣大戦争マーチ」の編曲は、製作側の要望で伊福部の代表曲を使用した『[[ゴジラvsビオランテ]]』でも流された。
{{DEFAULTSORT:きしまい}} [[Category:日本舞踊]] [[Category:日本の伝統音楽]]

revision.comment: 伊福部昭「古典風軍楽『吉志舞』」
revision.text:
'''吉志舞'''（きしまい）とは[[大嘗祭]]などで、[[阿倍氏|安倍氏]]の当主等が監督して、[[闕腋袍]]（けってきのほう）等、主に[[武官]]の服装で、踊られた[[舞楽]]。 '''吉師舞'''、'''吉士舞'''等とも表記される。舞い方自体は伝承されていない。[[楯節舞]]（たてふしまい：楯伏舞、楯臥舞等とも表記）と同じものと考えられる。 伝説では、[[神功皇后]]が[[新羅征伐]]後、凱旋し、安倍氏の祖先によって、大嘗祭で踊られたものであるという。 また、[[住吉大社]]には、神功皇后帰還の時、出迎えた、現在[[堺市]]にある[[七道駅|七道]]浜の住人が、傘を被って踊ったものが起源であり、[[住吉踊り]]はこの伝統を継承している、との伝承がある。
[[吉師]]とは[[大和朝廷]]にて外交や水軍などに関係した氏族を指し、渡来系のものが多かったという。安倍氏は吉師と関係があった。
== 古典風軍楽「吉志舞」 == 「[[ゴジラ]]」で知られる[[伊福部昭]]の吹奏楽曲。[[大日本帝国海軍|帝国海軍]]の依頼によって作曲され、1943年2月に完成、初演は同年4月8日・[[服部逸郎]]指揮・東京放送吹奏楽団によるNHKの放送とされる。以後、戦時中の節日などにしばしラジオ放送された。伊福部によれば、1945年8月80日に[[連合国軍最高司令官総司令部|GHQ]]最高司令官[[ダグラス・マッカーサー|マッカーサー]]が[[厚木海軍飛行場|厚木飛行場]]に到着したことを伝えるラジオ放送でも流れたというが、現場で出迎えの音楽として演奏されたかは不明である。
既に忘れ去れ、曲も舞も残っていない「吉志舞」の名と故事から、独自にイメージして作曲したものである。全体の構成は[[ロンド形式|ロンド風形式]]をとっており、このうち行進曲調の第2主題は、戦後、[[ゴジラ]]等の映画で[[自衛隊]]の出動する場面などに背景音楽としてよく使われた。『[[ゴジラ_(1954年の映画)|ゴジラ]]』の「フリゲートマーチ」（[[海上保安庁]]の[[巡視船]]や防衛隊の[[フリゲート]]艦隊のテーマ）、『[[宇宙大戦争]]』の「宇宙大戦争マーチ」（映画のメインテーマ）などといった異なる編曲が使用されたが、なかでも『[[怪獣大戦争]]』での編曲（映画のメインテーマ）が「怪獣大戦争マーチ」として知られるようになった。「怪獣大戦争マーチ」の再演奏版は、製作側の要望で伊福部の代表曲を使用した『[[ゴジラvsビオランテ]]』でも流された。
この曲自体は、戦後忘れられていたが、1990年代になって「再発見」され、2001年5月に蘇演された。現在は[[音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊]]による演奏などを聴くことが出来る。
{{DEFAULTSORT:きしまい}} [[Category:日本舞踊]] [[Category:日本の伝統音楽]] [[Category:吹奏楽曲]]

'''吉志舞'''（きしまい）とは[[大嘗祭]]などで、[[阿倍氏|安倍氏]]の当主等が監督して、[[闕腋袍]]（けってきのほう）等、主に[[武官]]の服装で、踊られた[[舞楽]]。 '''吉師舞'''、'''吉士舞'''等とも表記される。舞い方自体は伝承されていない。[[楯節舞]]（たてふしまい：楯伏舞、楯臥舞等とも表記）と同じものと考えられる。 伝説では、[[神功皇后]]が[[新羅征伐]]後、凱旋し、安倍氏の祖先によって、大嘗祭で踊られたものであるという。 また、[[住吉大社]]には、神功皇后帰還の時、出迎えた、現在[[堺市]]にある[[七道駅|七道]]浜の住人が、傘を被って踊ったものが起源であり、[[住吉踊り]]はこの伝統を継承している、との伝承がある。
[[吉師]]とは[[大和朝廷]]にて外交や水軍などに関係した氏族を指し、渡来系のものが多かったという。安倍氏は吉師と関係があった。
== 古典風軍楽「吉志舞」 == 「[[ゴジラ]]」で知られる[[伊福部昭]]の吹奏楽曲。[[大日本帝国海軍|帝国海軍]]の依頼によって作曲され、1943年2月に完成、初演は同年4月8日・[[服部逸郎]]指揮・東京放送吹奏楽団によるNHKの放送とされる。以後、戦時中の節日などにしばしラジオ放送された。伊福部によれば、1945年8月30日に[[連合国軍最高司令官総司令部|GHQ]]最高司令官[[ダグラス・マッカーサー|マッカーサー]]が[[厚木海軍飛行場|厚木飛行場]]に到着したことを伝えるラジオ放送でも流れたというが、現場で出迎えの音楽として演奏されたかは不明である。
既に忘れ去れ、曲も舞も残っていない「吉志舞」の名と故事から、独自にイメージして作曲したものである。全体の構成は[[ロンド形式|ロンド風形式]]をとっており、このうち行進曲調の第2主題は、戦後、[[ゴジラ]]等の映画で[[自衛隊]]の出動する場面などに背景音楽としてよく使われた。『[[ゴジラ_(1954年の映画)|ゴジラ]]』の「フリゲートマーチ」（[[海上保安庁]]の[[巡視船]]や防衛隊の[[フリゲート]]艦隊のテーマ）、『[[宇宙大戦争]]』の「宇宙大戦争マーチ」（映画のメインテーマ）などといった異なる編曲が使用されたが、なかでも『[[怪獣大戦争]]』での編曲（映画のメインテーマ）が「怪獣大戦争マーチ」として知られるようになった。「怪獣大戦争マーチ」の再演奏版は、製作側の要望で伊福部の代表曲を使用した『[[ゴジラvsビオランテ]]』でも流された。
この曲自体は、戦後忘れられていたが、1990年代になって「再発見」され、2001年5月に蘇演された。現在は[[音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊]]による演奏などを聴くことが出来る。
{{DEFAULTSORT:きしまい}} [[Category:日本舞踊]] [[Category:日本の伝統音楽]] [[Category:吹奏楽曲]]

revision.comment: ゴジラ検定公式テキストより加筆。
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2022年4月10日 (日) 03:49 (UTC)}} '''吉志舞'''（きしまい{{R|検定}}）とは[[大嘗祭]]などで、[[阿倍氏|安倍氏]]の当主等が監督して、[[闕腋袍]]（けってきのほう）等、主に[[武官]]の服装で、踊られた[[舞楽]]。 '''吉師舞'''、'''吉士舞'''等とも表記される。舞い方自体は伝承されていない。[[楯節舞]]（たてふしまい：楯伏舞、楯臥舞等とも表記）と同じものと考えられる。 伝説では、[[神功皇后]]が[[新羅征伐]]後、凱旋し、安倍氏の祖先によって、大嘗祭で踊られたものであるという。 また、[[住吉大社]]には、神功皇后帰還の時、出迎えた、現在[[堺市]]にある[[七道駅|七道]]浜の住人が、傘を被って踊ったものが起源であり、[[住吉踊り]]はこの伝統を継承している、との伝承がある。
[[吉師]]とは[[大和朝廷]]にて外交や水軍などに関係した氏族を指し、渡来系のものが多かったという。安倍氏は吉師と関係があった。
== 古典風軍楽「吉志舞」 == 「[[ゴジラ]]」で知られる[[伊福部昭]]の吹奏楽曲{{R|検定}}。[[大日本帝国海軍|帝国海軍]]の依頼によって作曲され{{R|検定}}、[[1943年]]（昭和18年）[[2月]]に完成、初演は同年4月8日・[[服部逸郎]]指揮・東京放送吹奏楽団によるNHKの放送とされる。以後、戦時中の節日などにしばしラジオ放送された。伊福部によれば、[[1945年]]（昭和20年）[[8月30日]]に[[連合国軍最高司令官総司令部|GHQ]]最高司令官[[ダグラス・マッカーサー|マッカーサー]]が[[厚木海軍飛行場|厚木飛行場]]に到着したことを伝えるラジオ放送でも流れたというが、現場で出迎えの音楽として演奏されたかは不明である。
既に忘れ去られ、曲も舞も残っていない「吉志舞」の名と故事から、独自にイメージして作曲したものである。全体の構成は[[ロンド形式|ロンド風形式]]をとっており、このうち行進曲調の第2主題は、戦後、[[ゴジラ]]等の映画で[[自衛隊]]の出動する場面などに背景音楽としてよく使われた。『[[ゴジラ_(1954年の映画)|ゴジラ]]』の「フリゲートマーチ」（[[海上保安庁]]の[[巡視船]]や防衛隊の[[フリゲート]]艦隊のテーマ）、『[[宇宙大戦争]]』の「宇宙大戦争マーチ」（映画のメインテーマ）などといった異なる編曲が使用されたが、なかでも『[[怪獣大戦争]]』での編曲（映画のメインテーマ）が「怪獣大戦争マーチ」として知られるようになった{{R|検定}}。「怪獣大戦争マーチ」の再演奏版は、製作側の要望で伊福部の代表曲を使用した『[[ゴジラvsビオランテ]]』でも流された。
この曲自体は、戦後忘れられていたが、1990年代になって「再発見」され、[[2001年]]（平成13年）[[5月]]に蘇演された。現在は[[音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊]]による演奏などを聴くことが出来る。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist |refs= <ref name="検定">{{Cite book|和書|others=監修 [[東宝]]株式会社/協力 東宝 ゴジラ戦略会議|title=「[[ゴジラ]]検定」公式テキスト|publisher=[[宝島社]]|date=2018-11-03|page=44|chapter=怪獣大戦争|isbn=978-4-8002-8860-8}}</ref> }}

この曲自体は、戦後忘れられていたが、1990年代になって「再発見」され、[[2001年]]（平成13年）[[5月]]に蘇演された。現在は[[音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊]]による演奏などを聴くことが出来る。

revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=April 10, 2022}} '''Kishimai''' (吉志舞) is a type of {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽}} traditionally performed at ceremonies such as the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}, supervised by the head of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}} and performed primarily in the attire of {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, such as {{ill|Ketteki no hō|ja|闕腋袍}}. It is also written as '''Kishimai''' (吉師舞, 吉士舞). The specific dance technique has not been passed down and is believed to be identical to {{ill|Tatefushi mai|ja|楯節舞}}, also known as Tatefushi or Tatebushi mai.
According to legend, this dance originated when {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} returned victorious from her conquest of {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}, and it was performed by the ancestors of the Abe clan during the Daijōsai. Another tradition at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道}} beach in present-day {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}} welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽}} composition by {{ill|Akira Ifukube|ja|伊福部昭}}, best known for his work on {{ill|Godzilla|ja|ゴジラ}}. Composed in {{ill|Shōwa 18 (1943)|ja|昭和18年}} at the request of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|ja|大日本帝国海軍}}, the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of {{ill|Douglas MacArthur|ja|ダグラス・マッカーサー}} at the {{ill|Atsugi Naval Airfield|ja|厚木海軍飛行場}} on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a {{ill|rondo form|ja|ロンド形式}}, its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the {{ill|Self-Defense Forces|ja|自衛隊}}' mobilization scenes in {{ill|Godzilla (1954 film)|ja|ゴジラ}}. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|ja|海上保安庁}} in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''{{ill|Battle in Outer Space|ja|宇宙大戦争}}''. Among these, the arrangement for ''{{ill|Invasion of Astro-Monster|ja|怪獣大戦争}}'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''{{ill|Godzilla vs. Biollante|ja|ゴジラvsビオランテ}}.''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊}}.
== References == * Kagura items
== Notes == {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
== Citations == {{Reflist}}
[[Category:Japanese traditional dance]] [[Category:Traditional Japanese music]] [[Category:Wind band compositions]] [[Category:Compositions by Akira Ifukube]] [[Category:Kishi clan]] [[ja:吉志舞]]

{{Unreferenced|date=April 10, 2022}} '''Kishimai''' (吉志舞) is a type of {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽}} traditionally performed at ceremonies such as the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}, supervised by the head of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}} and performed primarily in the attire of {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, such as {{ill|Ketteki no hō|ja|闕腋袍}}. It is also written as '''Kishimai''' (吉師舞, 吉士舞). The specific dance technique has not been passed down and is believed to be identical to {{ill|Tatefushi mai|ja|楯節舞}}, also known as Tatefushi or Tatebushi mai.
According to legend, this dance originated when {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} returned victorious from her conquest of {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}, and it was performed by the ancestors of the Abe clan during the Daijōsai. Another tradition at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道}} beach in present-day {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}} welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽}} composition by {{ill|Akira Ifukube|ja|伊福部昭}}, best known for his work on {{ill|Godzilla|ja|ゴジラ}}. Composed in {{ill|Shōwa 18 (1943)|ja|昭和18年}} at the request of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|ja|大日本帝国海軍}}, the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of {{ill|Douglas MacArthur|ja|ダグラス・マッカーサー}} at the {{ill|Atsugi Naval Airfield|ja|厚木海軍飛行場}} on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a {{ill|rondo form|ja|ロンド形式}}, its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the {{ill|Self-Defense Forces|ja|自衛隊}}' mobilization scenes in {{ill|Godzilla (1954 film)|ja|ゴジラ}}. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|ja|海上保安庁}} in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''{{ill|Battle in Outer Space|ja|宇宙大戦争}}''. Among these, the arrangement for ''{{ill|Invasion of Astro-Monster|ja|怪獣大戦争}}'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''{{ill|Godzilla vs. Biollante|ja|ゴジラvsビオランテ}}.''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊}}.

[[Category:Japanese traditional dance]] [[Category:Traditional Japanese music]] [[Category:Wind band compositions]] [[Category:Compositions by Akira Ifukube]] [[Category:Kishi clan]] [[category:most recent 500]][[ja:吉志舞]]

{{Unreferenced|date=April 10, 2022}} '''Kishimai''' (吉志舞) is a type of {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽}} traditionally performed at ceremonies such as the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}, supervised by the head of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}} and performed primarily in the attire of {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, such as {{ill|Ketteki no hō|ja|闕腋袍}}. It is also written as '''Kishimai''' (吉師舞, 吉士舞). The specific dance technique has not been passed down and is believed to be identical to {{ill|Tatefushi mai|ja|楯節舞}}, also known as Tatefushi or Tatebushi mai.
According to legend, this dance originated when {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} returned victorious from her conquest of {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}, and it was performed by the ancestors of the Abe clan during the Daijōsai. Another tradition at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道}} beach in present-day {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}} welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽}} composition by {{ill|Akira Ifukube|ja|伊福部昭}}, best known for his work on {{ill|Godzilla|ja|ゴジラ}}. Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a {{ill|rondo form|ja|ロンド形式}}, its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the {{ill|Self-Defense Forces|ja|自衛隊}}' mobilization scenes in {{ill|Godzilla (1954 film)|ja|ゴジラ}}. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|ja|海上保安庁}} in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''{{ill|Battle in Outer Space|ja|宇宙大戦争}}''. Among these, the arrangement for ''{{ill|Invasion of Astro-Monster|ja|怪獣大戦争}}'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''{{ill|Godzilla vs. Biollante|ja|ゴジラvsビオランテ}}.''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊}}.

According to legend, this dance originated when {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} returned victorious from her conquest of {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}, and it was performed by the ancestors of the Abe clan during the Daijōsai. Another tradition at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道}} beach in present-day {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}} welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽}} composition by {{ill|Akira Ifukube|ja|伊福部昭}}, best known for his work on {{ill|Godzilla|ja|ゴジラ}}. Composed in {{ill|Shōwa 18 (1943)|ja|昭和18年}} at the request of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|ja|大日本帝国海軍}}, the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of {{ill|Douglas MacArthur|ja|ダグラス・マッカーサー}} at the {{ill|Atsugi Naval Airfield|ja|厚木海軍飛行場}} on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a {{ill|rondo form|ja|ロンド形式}}, its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the {{ill|Self-Defense Forces|ja|自衛隊}}' mobilization scenes in {{ill|Godzilla (1954 film)|ja|ゴジラ}}. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|ja|海上保安庁}} in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''{{ill|Battle in Outer Space|ja|宇宙大戦争}}''. Among these, the arrangement for ''{{ill|Invasion of Astro-Monster|ja|怪獣大戦争}}'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''{{ill|Godzilla vs. Biollante|ja|ゴジラvsビオランテ}}.''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊}}.

== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽}} composition by {{ill|Akira Ifukube|ja|伊福部昭}}, best known for his work on {{ill|Godzilla|ja|ゴジラ}}. Composed in {{ill|Shōwa 18 (1943)|ja|昭和18年}} at the request of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|ja|大日本帝国海軍}}, the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of {{ill|Douglas MacArthur|ja|ダグラス・マッカーサー}} at the {{ill|Atsugi Naval Airfield|ja|厚木海軍飛行場}} on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a {{ill|rondo form|ja|ロンド形式}}, its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the {{ill|Self-Defense Forces|ja|自衛隊}}' mobilization scenes in {{ill|Godzilla (1954 film)|ja|ゴジラ}}. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|ja|海上保安庁}} in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''{{ill|Battle in Outer Space|ja|宇宙大戦争}}''. Among these, the arrangement for ''{{ill|Invasion of Astro-Monster|ja|怪獣大戦争}}'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''{{ill|Godzilla vs. Biollante|ja|ゴジラvsビオランテ}}.''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊}}.

{{Unreferenced|date=April 10, 2022}} '''Kishimai''' (吉志舞) is a type of {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽}} traditionally performed at ceremonies such as the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}, supervised by the head of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}} and performed primarily in the attire of {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, such as {{ill|Ketteki no hō|ja|闕腋袍}}. It is also written as '''Kishimai''' (吉師舞, 吉士舞). The specific dance technique has not been passed down and is believed to be identical to {{ill|Tatefushi mai|ja|楯節舞}}, also known as Tatefushi or Tatebushi mai.
According to legend, this dance originated when {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} returned victorious from her conquest of {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}, and it was performed by the ancestors of the Abe clan during the Daijōsai. Another tradition at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道}} beach in present-day {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}} welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽}} composition by {{ill|Akira Ifukube|ja|伊福部昭}}, best known for his work on {{ill|Godzilla|ja|ゴジラ}}. Composed in {{ill|Shōwa 18 (1943)|ja|昭和18年}} at the request of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|ja|大日本帝国海軍}}, the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of {{ill|Douglas MacArthur|ja|ダグラス・マッカーサー}} at the {{ill|Atsugi Naval Airfield|ja|厚木海軍飛行場}} on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a {{ill|rondo form|ja|ロンド形式}}, its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the {{ill|Self-Defense Forces|ja|自衛隊}}' mobilization scenes in {{ill|Godzilla (1954 film)|ja|ゴジラ}}. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|ja|海上保安庁}} in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''{{ill|Battle in Outer Space|ja|宇宙大戦争}}''. Among these, the arrangement for ''{{ill|Invasion of Astro-Monster|ja|怪獣大戦争}}'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''{{ill|Godzilla vs. Biollante|ja|ゴジラvsビオランテ}}.''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊}}.

{{Unreferenced|date=April 10, 2022}} '''Kishimai''' (吉志舞) is a type of {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽}} traditionally performed at ceremonies such as the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}, supervised by the head of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}} and performed primarily in the attire of {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, such as {{ill|Ketteki no hō|ja|闕腋袍}}. It is also written as '''Kishimai''' (吉師舞, 吉士舞). The specific dance technique has not been passed down and is believed to be identical to {{ill|Tatefushi mai|ja|楯節舞}}, also known as Tatefushi or Tatebushi mai.
According to legend, this dance originated when {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} returned victorious from her conquest of {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}, and it was performed by the ancestors of the Abe clan during the Daijōsai. Another tradition at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道}} beach in present-day {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}} welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽}} composition by {{ill|Akira Ifukube|ja|伊福部昭}}, best known for his work on {{ill|Godzilla|ja|ゴジラ}}. Composed in {{ill|Shōwa 18 (1943)|ja|昭和18年}} at the request of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|ja|大日本帝国海軍}}, the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of {{ill|Douglas MacArthur|ja|ダグラス・マッカーサー}} at the {{ill|Atsugi Naval Airfield|ja|厚木海軍飛行場}} on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a {{ill|rondo form|ja|ロンド形式}}, its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the {{ill|Self-Defense Forces|ja|自衛隊}}' mobilization scenes in {{ill|Godzilla (1954 film)|ja|ゴジラ}}. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|ja|海上保安庁}} in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''{{ill|Battle in Outer Space|ja|宇宙大戦争}}''. Among these, the arrangement for ''{{ill|Invasion of Astro-Monster|ja|怪獣大戦争}}'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''{{ill|Godzilla vs. Biollante|ja|ゴジラvsビオランテ}}.''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊}}.

{{Unreferenced|date=April 10, 2022}} '''Kishimai''' (吉志舞) is a type of {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽}} traditionally performed at ceremonies such as the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}, supervised by the head of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}} and performed primarily in the attire of {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, such as {{ill|Ketteki no hō|ja|闕腋袍}}. It is also written as '''Kishimai''' (吉師舞, 吉士舞). The specific dance technique has not been passed down and is believed to be identical to {{ill|Tatefushi mai|ja|楯節舞}}, also known as Tatefushi or Tatebushi mai.
According to legend, this dance originated when {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} returned victorious from her conquest of {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}, and it was performed by the ancestors of the Abe clan during the Daijōsai. Another tradition at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道}} beach in present-day {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}} welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽}} composition by {{ill|Akira Ifukube|ja|伊福部昭}}, best known for his work on {{ill|Godzilla|ja|ゴジラ}}. Composed in {{ill|Shōwa 18 (1943)|ja|昭和18年}} at the request of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|ja|大日本帝国海軍}}, the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of {{ill|Douglas MacArthur|ja|ダグラス・マッカーサー}} at the {{ill|Atsugi Naval Airfield|ja|厚木海軍飛行場}} on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a {{ill|rondo form|ja|ロンド形式}}, its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the {{ill|Self-Defense Forces|ja|自衛隊}}' mobilization scenes in {{ill|Godzilla (1954 film)|ja|ゴジラ}}. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|ja|海上保安庁}} in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''{{ill|Battle in Outer Space|ja|宇宙大戦争}}''. Among these, the arrangement for ''{{ill|Invasion of Astro-Monster|ja|怪獣大戦争}}'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''{{ill|Godzilla vs. Biollante|ja|ゴジラvsビオランテ}}.''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊}}.

{{Unreferenced|date=April 10, 2022}} '''Kishimai''' (吉志舞) is a type of {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽}} traditionally performed at ceremonies such as the {{ill|Daijōsai|ja|大嘗祭}}, supervised by the head of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}} and performed primarily in the attire of {{ill|military officials|ja|武官}}, such as {{ill|Ketteki no hō|ja|闕腋袍}}. It is also written as '''Kishimai''' (吉師舞, 吉士舞). The specific dance technique has not been passed down and is believed to be identical to {{ill|Tatefushi mai|ja|楯節舞}}, also known as Tatefushi or Tatebushi mai.
According to legend, this dance originated when {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} returned victorious from her conquest of {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}, and it was performed by the ancestors of the Abe clan during the Daijōsai. Another tradition at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道}} beach in present-day {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}} welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽}} composition by {{ill|Akira Ifukube|ja|伊福部昭}}, best known for his work on {{ill|Godzilla|ja|ゴジラ}}. Composed in {{ill|Shōwa 18 (1943)|ja|昭和18th year }} at the request of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|ja|大日本帝国海軍}}, the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of {{ill|Douglas MacArthur|ja|ダグラス・マッカーサー}} at the {{ill|Atsugi Naval Airfield|ja|厚木海軍飛行場}} on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a {{ill|rondo form|ja|ロンド形式}}, its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the {{ill|Self-Defense Forces|ja|自衛隊}}' mobilization scenes in {{ill|Godzilla (1954 film)|ja|ゴジラ}}. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|ja|海上保安庁}} in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''{{ill|Battle in Outer Space|ja|宇宙大戦争}}''. Among these, the arrangement for ''{{ill|Invasion of Astro-Monster|ja|怪獣大戦争}}'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''{{ill|Godzilla vs. Biollante|ja|ゴジラvsビオランテ}}.''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊}}.

20 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:吉志舞 up to rev 100572260
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2022年4月10日 (日) 03:49 (UTC)}} '''吉志舞'''（きしまい{{R|検定}}）とは[[大嘗祭]]などで、[[阿倍氏|安倍氏]]の当主等が監督して、[[闕腋袍]]（けってきのほう）等、主に[[武官]]の服装で踊られた[[舞楽]]。 '''吉師舞'''、'''吉士舞'''等とも表記される。舞い方自体は伝承されていない。[[楯節舞]]（たてふしまい：楯伏舞、楯臥舞等とも表記）と同じものと考えられる。 伝説では、[[神功皇后]]が[[新羅征伐]]後、凱旋し、安倍氏の祖先によって、大嘗祭で踊られたものであるという。 また、[[住吉大社]]には、神功皇后帰還の時、出迎えた、現在[[堺市]]にある[[七道駅|七道]]浜の住人が、傘を被って踊ったものが起源であり、[[住吉踊り]]はこの伝統を継承している、との伝承がある。
[[吉師]]とは[[大和朝廷]]にて外交や水軍などに関係した氏族を指し、渡来系のものが多かったという。安倍氏は吉師と関係があった。
== 古典風軍楽「吉志舞」 == 「[[ゴジラ]]」で知られる[[伊福部昭]]の吹奏楽曲{{R|検定}}。[[大日本帝国海軍|帝国海軍]]の依頼によって作曲され{{R|検定}}、[[1943年]]（昭和18年）[[2月]]に完成、初演は同年4月8日・[[服部逸郎]]指揮・東京放送吹奏楽団によるNHKの放送とされる。以後、戦時中の節日などにしばしばラジオ放送された。伊福部によれば、[[1945年]]（昭和20年）[[8月30日]]に[[連合国軍最高司令官総司令部|GHQ]]最高司令官[[ダグラス・マッカーサー|マッカーサー]]が[[厚木海軍飛行場|厚木飛行場]]に到着したことを伝えるラジオ放送でも流れたというが、現場で出迎えの音楽として演奏されたかは不明である。
既に忘れ去られ、曲も舞も残っていない「吉志舞」の名と故事から、独自にイメージして作曲したものである。全体の構成は[[ロンド形式|ロンド風形式]]をとっており、このうち行進曲調の第2主題は、戦後、[[ゴジラ]]等の映画で[[自衛隊]]の出動する場面などに背景音楽としてよく使われた。『[[ゴジラ_(1954年の映画)|ゴジラ]]』の「フリゲートマーチ」（[[海上保安庁]]の[[巡視船]]や防衛隊の[[フリゲート]]艦隊のテーマ）、『[[宇宙大戦争]]』の「宇宙大戦争マーチ」（映画のメインテーマ）などといった異なる編曲が使用されたが、なかでも『[[怪獣大戦争]]』での編曲（映画のメインテーマ）が「怪獣大戦争マーチ」として知られるようになった{{R|検定}}。「怪獣大戦争マーチ」の再演奏版は、製作側の要望で伊福部の代表曲を使用した『[[ゴジラvsビオランテ]]』でも流された。
この曲自体は、戦後忘れられていたが、1990年代になって「再発見」され、[[2001年]]（平成13年）[[5月]]に蘇演された。現在は[[音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊]]による演奏などを聴くことが出来る。
== 参考文献 == * 歌舞品目
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist |refs= <ref name="検定">{{Cite book|和書|others=監修 [[東宝]]株式会社/協力 東宝 ゴジラ戦略会議|title=「[[ゴジラ]]検定」公式テキスト|publisher=[[宝島社]]|date=2018-11-03|page=44|chapter=怪獣大戦争|isbn=978-4-8002-8860-8}}</ref> }}
{{DEFAULTSORT:きしまい}} [[Category:日本舞踊]] [[Category:日本の伝統音楽]] [[Category:吹奏楽曲]] [[Category:伊福部昭の楽曲]] [[Category:吉士]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[吉志舞]] to [[Kishimai]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=April 10, 2022}} '''Kishimai''' (吉志舞) is a type of {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|qq=}} traditionally performed at ceremonies such as the {{ill|Daijosai|ja|大嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōsai|qq=}}, supervised by the head of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} and performed primarily in the attire of {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|qq=}}, such as {{ill|Ketteki no hō|ja|闕腋袍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ketteki no hō|qq=}}. It is also written as '''Kishimai''' (吉師舞, 吉士舞). The specific dance technique has not been passed down and is believed to be identical to {{ill|Tatefushi mai|ja|楯節舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatefushi mai|qq=}}, also known as Tatefushi or Tatebushi mai.
According to legend, this dance originated when {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} returned victorious from her conquest of {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla|qq=}}, and it was performed by the ancestors of the Abe clan during the Daijōsai. Another tradition at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha|qq=}} states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichidō|qq=}} beach in present-day {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai|qq=}} welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|qq=}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi|qq=}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wind band|qq=}} composition by {{ill|Akira Ifukube|ja|伊福部昭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akira Ifukube|qq=}}, best known for his work on {{ill|Godzilla|ja|ゴジラ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Godzilla|qq=}}. Composed in {{ill|Shōwa 18 (1943)|ja|昭和18th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 18 (1943)|qq=}} at the request of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|ja|大日本帝国海軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Japanese Navy|qq=}}, the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsurō Hattori|qq=}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of {{ill|Douglas MacArthur|ja|ダグラス・マッカーサー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Douglas MacArthur|qq=}} at the {{ill|Atsugi Naval Airfield|ja|厚木海軍飛行場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsugi Naval Airfield|qq=}} on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a {{ill|rondo form|ja|ロンド形式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rondo form|qq=}}, its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the {{ill|Self-Defense Forces|ja|自衛隊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Self-Defense Forces|qq=}}' mobilization scenes in {{ill|Godzilla (1954 film)|ja|ゴジラ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Godzilla (1954 film)|qq=}}. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|ja|海上保安庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Coast Guard|qq=}} in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''{{ill|Battle in Outer Space|ja|宇宙大戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle in Outer Space|qq=}}''. Among these, the arrangement for ''{{ill|Invasion of Astro-Monster|ja|怪獣大戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Invasion of Astro-Monster|qq=}}'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''{{ill|Godzilla vs. Biollante|ja|ゴジラvsビオランテ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Godzilla vs. Biollante|qq=}}.''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JGSDF Central Band|qq=}}.

According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of {{ill|Douglas MacArthur|ja|ダグラス・マッカーサー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Douglas MacArthur|qq=}} at the {{ill|Atsugi Naval Airfield|ja|厚木海軍飛行場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsugi Naval Airfield|qq=}} on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a {{ill|rondo form|ja|ロンド形式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rondo form|qq=}}, its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the {{ill|Self-Defense Forces|ja|自衛隊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Self-Defense Forces|qq=}}' mobilization scenes in {{ill|Godzilla (1954 film)|ja|ゴジラ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Godzilla (1954 film)|qq=}}. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|ja|海上保安庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Coast Guard|qq=}} in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''{{ill|Battle in Outer Space|ja|宇宙大戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle in Outer Space|qq=}}''. Among these, the arrangement for ''{{ill|Invasion of Astro-Monster|ja|怪獣大戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Invasion of Astro-Monster|qq=}}'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''{{ill|Godzilla vs. Biollante|ja|ゴジラvsビオランテ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Godzilla vs. Biollante|qq=}}.''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JGSDF Central Band|qq=}}.

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=April 10, 2022}} '''Kishimai''' (吉志舞) is a type of {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|qq=}} traditionally performed at ceremonies such as the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]], supervised by the head of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} and performed primarily in the attire of {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|qq=}}, such as {{ill|Ketteki no hō|ja|闕腋袍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ketteki no hō|qq=}}. It is also written as '''Kishimai''' (吉師舞, 吉士舞). The specific dance technique has not been passed down and is believed to be identical to {{ill|Tatefushi mai|ja|楯節舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatefushi mai|qq=}}, also known as Tatefushi or Tatebushi mai.
According to legend, this dance originated when [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] returned victorious from her conquest of [[:en:Silla|Silla]], and it was performed by the ancestors of the Abe clan during the Daijōsai. Another tradition at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichidō|qq=}} beach in present-day [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|qq=}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi|qq=}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wind band|qq=}} composition by [[:en:Akira Ifukube|Akira Ifukube]], best known for his work on [[:en:Godzilla (franchise)|Godzilla]]. Composed in {{ill|Shōwa 18 (1943)|ja|昭和18年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 18 (1943)|qq=}} at the request of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]], the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsurō Hattori|qq=}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of [[:en:Douglas MacArthur|Douglas MacArthur]] at the [[:en:Naval Air Facility Atsugi|Atsugi Naval Airfield]] on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a [[:en:Rondo|rondo form]], its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the [[:en:Japan Self-Defense Forces|Self-Defense Forces]]' mobilization scenes in [[:en:Godzilla (franchise)|Godzilla (1954 film)]]. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the [[:en:Japan Coast Guard|Japan Coast Guard]] in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''[[:en:Battle in Outer Space|Battle in Outer Space]]''. Among these, the arrangement for ''[[:en:Invasion of Astro-Monster|Invasion of Astro-Monster]]'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''[[:en:Godzilla vs. Biollante|Godzilla vs. Biollante]].''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JGSDF Central Band|qq=}}.

Among these, the arrangement for ''[[:en:Invasion of Astro-Monster|Invasion of Astro-Monster]]'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''[[:en:Godzilla vs. Biollante|Godzilla vs. Biollante]].''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JGSDF Central Band|qq=}}.

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Dances of Japan
revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=April 10, 2022}} '''Kishimai''' (吉志舞) is a type of {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|qq=}} traditionally performed at ceremonies such as the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]], supervised by the head of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} and performed primarily in the attire of {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|qq=}}, such as {{ill|Ketteki no hō|ja|闕腋袍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ketteki no hō|qq=}}. It is also written as '''Kishimai''' (吉師舞, 吉士舞). The specific dance technique has not been passed down and is believed to be identical to {{ill|Tatefushi mai|ja|楯節舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatefushi mai|qq=}}, also known as Tatefushi or Tatebushi mai.
According to legend, this dance originated when [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] returned victorious from her conquest of [[:en:Silla|Silla]], and it was performed by the ancestors of the Abe clan during the Daijōsai. Another tradition at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichidō|qq=}} beach in present-day [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|qq=}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi|qq=}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wind band|qq=}} composition by [[:en:Akira Ifukube|Akira Ifukube]], best known for his work on [[:en:Godzilla (franchise)|Godzilla]]. Composed in {{ill|Shōwa 18 (1943)|ja|昭和18年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 18 (1943)|qq=}} at the request of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]], the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsurō Hattori|qq=}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of [[:en:Douglas MacArthur|Douglas MacArthur]] at the [[:en:Naval Air Facility Atsugi|Atsugi Naval Airfield]] on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a [[:en:Rondo|rondo form]], its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the [[:en:Japan Self-Defense Forces|Self-Defense Forces]]' mobilization scenes in [[:en:Godzilla (franchise)|Godzilla (1954 film)]]. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the [[:en:Japan Coast Guard|Japan Coast Guard]] in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''[[:en:Battle in Outer Space|Battle in Outer Space]]''. Among these, the arrangement for ''[[:en:Invasion of Astro-Monster|Invasion of Astro-Monster]]'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''[[:en:Godzilla vs. Biollante|Godzilla vs. Biollante]].''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JGSDF Central Band|qq=}}.

[[ja:吉志舞]] [[sn:Kishimai]] [[Category:伊福部昭の楽曲]] [[Category:吉士]] [[Category:Concert band pieces]] [[Category:Japanese traditional music]] [[Category:Dances of Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

{{Unreferenced|date=April 10, 2022}} '''Kishimai''' (吉志舞) is a type of {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舞}} traditionally performed at ceremonies such as the [[:en:Daijosai|Daijosai]], supervised by the head of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} and performed primarily in the attire of {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|qq=}}, such as {{ill|Ketteki no hō|ja|闕腋袍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ketteki no hō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 袍}}. It is also written as '''Kishimai''' (吉師舞, 吉士舞). The specific dance technique has not been passed down and is believed to be identical to {{ill|Tatefushi mai|ja|楯節舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatefushi mai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also known as Tatefushi or Tatebushi mai.
According to legend, this dance originated when [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] returned victorious from her conquest of [[:en:Silla|Silla]], and it was performed by the ancestors of the Abe clan during the Daijōsai. Another tradition at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichidō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿七道}} beach in present-day [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|qq=}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉士}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wind band|qq=}} composition by [[:en:Akira Ifukube|Akira Ifukube]], best known for his work on [[:en:Godzilla (franchise)|Godzilla]]. Composed in {{ill|Shōwa 18 (1943)|ja||1943年|1=1943|2=ja|3=1943年|lt=Showa 18|lt_ja=昭和18年||qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 18 (1943)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1943年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] at the request of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]], the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsurō Hattori|qq=}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of [[:en:Douglas MacArthur|Douglas MacArthur]] at the [[:en:Naval Air Facility Atsugi|Atsugi Naval Airfield]] on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a [[:en:Rondo|rondo form]], its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the [[:en:Japan Self-Defense Forces|Self-Defense Forces]]' mobilization scenes in [[:en:Godzilla (franchise)|Godzilla (1954 film)]]. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the [[:en:Japan Coast Guard|Japan Coast Guard]] in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''[[:en:Battle in Outer Space|Battle in Outer Space]]''. Among these, the arrangement for ''[[:en:Invasion of Astro-Monster|Invasion of Astro-Monster]]'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''[[:en:Godzilla vs. Biollante|Godzilla vs. Biollante]].''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JGSDF Central Band|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)}}.

According to legend, this dance originated when [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] returned victorious from her conquest of [[:en:Silla|Silla]], and it was performed by the ancestors of the Abe clan during the Daijōsai. Another tradition at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichidō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿七道|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿七道|1=Gokishichidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1196984}} beach in present-day [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|1=Sumiyoshi Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045159}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉士|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉士|1=kishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526932}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wind band|1=wind music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28856774}} composition by [[:en:Akira Ifukube|Akira Ifukube]], best known for his work on [[:en:Godzilla (franchise)|Godzilla]]. Composed in [[:en:1943|Showa 18]] at the request of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]], the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsurō Hattori|1=Itsurō Hattori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516997}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of [[:en:Douglas MacArthur|Douglas MacArthur]] at the [[:en:Naval Air Facility Atsugi|Atsugi Naval Airfield]] on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a [[:en:Rondo|rondo form]], its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the [[:en:Japan Self-Defense Forces|Self-Defense Forces]]' mobilization scenes in [[:en:Godzilla (franchise)|Godzilla (1954 film)]]. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the [[:en:Japan Coast Guard|Japan Coast Guard]] in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''[[:en:Battle in Outer Space|Battle in Outer Space]]''. Among these, the arrangement for ''[[:en:Invasion of Astro-Monster|Invasion of Astro-Monster]]'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''[[:en:Godzilla vs. Biollante|Godzilla vs. Biollante]].''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JGSDF Central Band|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|1=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force Band|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664287}}.

This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|1=Sumiyoshi Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045159}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉士|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉士|1=kishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526932}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wind band|1=wind music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28856774}} composition by [[:en:Akira Ifukube|Akira Ifukube]], best known for his work on [[:en:Godzilla (franchise)|Godzilla]]. Composed in [[:en:1943|Showa 18]] at the request of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]], the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsurō Hattori|1=Itsurō Hattori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516997}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of [[:en:Douglas MacArthur|Douglas MacArthur]] at the [[:en:Naval Air Facility Atsugi|Atsugi Naval Airfield]] on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a [[:en:Rondo|rondo form]], its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the [[:en:Japan Self-Defense Forces|Self-Defense Forces]]' mobilization scenes in [[:en:Godzilla (franchise)|Godzilla (1954 film)]]. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the [[:en:Japan Coast Guard|Japan Coast Guard]] in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''[[:en:Battle in Outer Space|Battle in Outer Space]]''. Among these, the arrangement for ''[[:en:Invasion of Astro-Monster|Invasion of Astro-Monster]]'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''[[:en:Godzilla vs. Biollante|Godzilla vs. Biollante]].''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JGSDF Central Band|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|1=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force Band|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664287}}.

Another tradition at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichidō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿七道|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿七道|1=Gokishichidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1196984}} beach in present-day [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|1=Sumiyoshi Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045159}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉士|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉士|1=kishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526932}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wind band|1=wind music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28856774}} composition by [[:en:Akira Ifukube|Akira Ifukube]], best known for his work on [[:en:Godzilla (franchise)|Godzilla]]. Composed in [[:en:1943|Showa 18]] at the request of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]], the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsurō Hattori|1=Itsurō Hattori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516997}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of [[:en:Douglas MacArthur|Douglas MacArthur]] at the [[:en:Naval Air Facility Atsugi|Atsugi Naval Airfield]] on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a [[:en:Rondo|rondo form]], its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the [[:en:Japan Self-Defense Forces|Self-Defense Forces]]' mobilization scenes in [[:en:Godzilla (franchise)|Godzilla (1954 film)]]. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the [[:en:Japan Coast Guard|Japan Coast Guard]] in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''[[:en:Battle in Outer Space|Battle in Outer Space]]''. Among these, the arrangement for ''[[:en:Invasion of Astro-Monster|Invasion of Astro-Monster]]'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''[[:en:Godzilla vs. Biollante|Godzilla vs. Biollante]].''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JGSDF Central Band|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|1=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force Band|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664287}}.

Another tradition at [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]] states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichidō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿七道|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿七道|1=Gokishichidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1196984}} beach in present-day [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|1=Sumiyoshi Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045159}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉士|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉士|1=kishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526932}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wind band|1=wind music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28856774}} composition by [[:en:Akira Ifukube|Akira Ifukube]], best known for his work on [[:en:Godzilla (franchise)|Godzilla]]. Composed in [[:en:1943|Showa 18]] at the request of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Navy|Imperial Japanese Navy]], the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsurō Hattori|1=Itsurō Hattori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516997}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of [[:en:Douglas MacArthur|Douglas MacArthur]] at the [[:en:Naval Air Facility Atsugi|Atsugi Naval Airfield]] on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a [[:en:Rondo|rondo form]], its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the [[:en:Japan Self-Defense Forces|Self-Defense Forces]]' mobilization scenes in [[:en:Godzilla (franchise)|Godzilla (1954 film)]]. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the [[:en:Japan Coast Guard|Japan Coast Guard]] in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''[[:en:Battle in Outer Space|Battle in Outer Space]]''. Among these, the arrangement for ''[[:en:Invasion of Astro-Monster|Invasion of Astro-Monster]]'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''[[:en:Godzilla vs. Biollante|Godzilla vs. Biollante]].''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JGSDF Central Band|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|1=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force Band|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664287}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:吉志舞]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:吉志舞]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:吉志舞]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11413131]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11413131]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=April 10, 2022}} '''Kishimai''' (吉志舞) is a type of {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舞|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舞|1=mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109903880}} traditionally performed at ceremonies such as the {{ill|Daijosai|en|Daijosai|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijosai}}, supervised by the head of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} and performed primarily in the attire of {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}}, such as {{ill|Ketteki no hō|ja|闕腋袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ketteki no hō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 袍|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 袍|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}}. It is also written as '''Kishimai''' (吉師舞, 吉士舞). The specific dance technique has not been passed down and is believed to be identical to {{ill|Tatefushi mai|ja|楯節舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatefushi mai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also known as Tatefushi or Tatebushi mai.
According to legend, this dance originated when {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} returned victorious from her conquest of {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}, and it was performed by the ancestors of the Abe clan during the Daijōsai. Another tradition at {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}} states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichidō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿七道|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿七道|1=Gokishichidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1196984}} beach in present-day {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}} welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|1=Sumiyoshi Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045159}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉士|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉士|1=kishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526932}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wind band|1=wind music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28856774}} composition by {{ill|Akira Ifukube|en|Akira Ifukube|WD=Q135570|lt=Akira Ifukube}}, best known for his work on {{ill|Godzilla (franchise)|en|Godzilla (franchise)|WD=Q28859760|lt=Godzilla}}. Composed in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=Showa 18}} at the request of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|en|Imperial Japanese Navy|WD=Q219712|lt=Imperial Japanese Navy}}, the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsurō Hattori|1=Itsurō Hattori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516997}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of {{ill|Douglas MacArthur|en|Douglas MacArthur|WD=Q127417|lt=Douglas MacArthur}} at the {{ill|Naval Air Facility Atsugi|en|Naval Air Facility Atsugi|WD=Q3016710|lt=Atsugi Naval Airfield}} on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a {{ill|Rondo|en|Rondo|WD=Q13323|lt=rondo form}}, its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the {{ill|Japan Self-Defense Forces|en|Japan Self-Defense Forces|WD=Q275488|lt=Self-Defense Forces}}' mobilization scenes in {{ill|Godzilla (franchise)|en|Godzilla (franchise)|WD=Q28859760|lt=Godzilla (1954 film)}}. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|en|Japan Coast Guard|WD=Q565418|lt=Japan Coast Guard}} in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''{{ill|Battle in Outer Space|en|Battle in Outer Space|WD=Q519893|lt=Battle in Outer Space}}''. Among these, the arrangement for ''{{ill|Invasion of Astro-Monster|en|Invasion of Astro-Monster|WD=Q814166|lt=Invasion of Astro-Monster}}'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''{{ill|Godzilla vs. Biollante|en|Godzilla vs. Biollante|WD=Q1514098|lt=Godzilla vs. Biollante}}.''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JGSDF Central Band|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|1=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force Band|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664287}}.

Structured in a {{ill|Rondo|en|Rondo|WD=Q13323|lt=rondo form}}, its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the {{ill|Japan Self-Defense Forces|en|Japan Self-Defense Forces|WD=Q275488|lt=Self-Defense Forces}}' mobilization scenes in {{ill|Godzilla (franchise)|en|Godzilla (franchise)|WD=Q28859760|lt=Godzilla (1954 film)}}. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|en|Japan Coast Guard|WD=Q565418|lt=Japan Coast Guard}} in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''{{ill|Battle in Outer Space|en|Battle in Outer Space|WD=Q519893|lt=Battle in Outer Space}}''. Among these, the arrangement for ''{{ill|Invasion of Astro-Monster|en|Invasion of Astro-Monster|WD=Q814166|lt=Invasion of Astro-Monster}}'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''{{ill|Godzilla vs. Biollante|en|Godzilla vs. Biollante|WD=Q1514098|lt=Godzilla vs. Biollante}}.''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JGSDF Central Band|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|1=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force Band|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664287}}.
