<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:吉志舞]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:吉志舞]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:吉志舞]]
{{Unreferenced|date=April 10, 2022}} '''Kishimai''' (吉志舞) is a type of {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舞|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舞|1=mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109903880}} traditionally performed at ceremonies such as the {{ill|Daijosai|en|Daijosai|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijosai|1=Daijosai}}, supervised by the head of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} and performed primarily in the attire of {{ill|military officials|ja|武官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military officials|1=military official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19598808}}, such as {{ill|Ketteki no hō|ja|闕腋袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ketteki no hō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 袍|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 袍|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}}. It is also written as '''Kishimai''' (吉師舞, 吉士舞). The specific dance technique has not been passed down and is believed to be identical to {{ill|Tatefushi mai|ja|楯節舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatefushi mai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also known as Tatefushi or Tatebushi mai.
According to legend, this dance originated when {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} returned victorious from her conquest of {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}, and it was performed by the ancestors of the Abe clan during the Daijōsai. Another tradition at {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichidō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿七道|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿七道|1=Gokishichidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1196984}} beach in present-day {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai|1=Sakai}} welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|1=Sumiyoshi Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045159}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉士|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉士|1=kishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526932}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wind band|1=wind music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28856774}} composition by {{ill|Akira Ifukube|en|Akira Ifukube|WD=Q135570|lt=Akira Ifukube|1=Akira Ifukube}}, best known for his work on {{ill|Godzilla (franchise)|en|Godzilla (franchise)|WD=Q28859760|lt=Godzilla|1=Godzilla (franchise)}}. Composed in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=Showa 18|1=1943}} at the request of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|en|Imperial Japanese Navy|WD=Q219712|lt=Imperial Japanese Navy|1=Imperial Japanese Navy}}, the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsurō Hattori|1=Itsurō Hattori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516997}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of {{ill|Douglas MacArthur|en|Douglas MacArthur|WD=Q127417|lt=Douglas MacArthur|1=Douglas MacArthur}} at the {{ill|Naval Air Facility Atsugi|en|Naval Air Facility Atsugi|WD=Q3016710|lt=Atsugi Naval Airfield|1=Naval Air Facility Atsugi}} on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a {{ill|Rondo|en|Rondo|WD=Q13323|lt=rondo form|1=Rondo}}, its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the {{ill|Japan Self-Defense Forces|en|Japan Self-Defense Forces|WD=Q275488|lt=Self-Defense Forces|1=Japan Self-Defense Forces}}' mobilization scenes in {{ill|Godzilla (franchise)|en|Godzilla (franchise)|WD=Q28859760|lt=Godzilla (1954 film)|1=Godzilla (franchise)}}. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|en|Japan Coast Guard|WD=Q565418|lt=Japan Coast Guard|1=Japan Coast Guard}} in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''{{ill|Battle in Outer Space|en|Battle in Outer Space|WD=Q519893|lt=Battle in Outer Space|1=Battle in Outer Space}}''. Among these, the arrangement for ''{{ill|Invasion of Astro-Monster|en|Invasion of Astro-Monster|WD=Q814166|lt=Invasion of Astro-Monster|1=Invasion of Astro-Monster}}'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''{{ill|Godzilla vs. Biollante|en|Godzilla vs. Biollante|WD=Q1514098|lt=Godzilla vs. Biollante|1=Godzilla vs. Biollante}}.''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JGSDF Central Band|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|1=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force Music Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664287}}.

The specific dance technique has not been passed down and is believed to be identical to {{ill|Tatefushi mai|ja|楯節舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatefushi mai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also known as Tatefushi or Tatebushi mai.
According to legend, this dance originated when {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} returned victorious from her conquest of {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}, and it was performed by the ancestors of the Abe clan during the Daijōsai. Another tradition at {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} states that this dance originated when residents of the {{ill|Shichidō|ja|七道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichidō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿七道|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿七道|1=Gokishichidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1196984}} beach in present-day {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai|1=Sakai}} welcomed the returning Empress Jingū by dancing with umbrellas. This tradition is believed to have evolved into the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|ja|住吉踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Odori|1=Sumiyoshi Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045159}}.
The term {{ill|Kishi|ja|吉師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉士|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉士|1=kishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526932}} refers to clans involved in diplomacy and naval activities during the Yamato court era. Many of these clans were of foreign origin and were associated with the Abe clan.
== Classical Military Music "Kishimai" == '''Kishimai''' is also the title of a {{ill|wind band|ja|吹奏楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wind band|1=wind music|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28856774}} composition by {{ill|Akira Ifukube|en|Akira Ifukube|WD=Q135570|lt=Akira Ifukube|1=Akira Ifukube}}, best known for his work on {{ill|Godzilla (franchise)|en|Godzilla (franchise)|WD=Q28859760|lt=Godzilla|1=Godzilla (franchise)}}. Composed in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=Showa 18|1=1943}} at the request of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|en|Imperial Japanese Navy|WD=Q219712|lt=Imperial Japanese Navy|1=Imperial Japanese Navy}}, the piece was completed in February of that year and premiered on April 8 under the baton of {{ill|Itsurō Hattori|ja|服部逸郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsurō Hattori|1=Itsurō Hattori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516997}}, performed by the Tokyo Broadcast Wind Orchestra on NHK Radio.
According to Ifukube, the piece was reportedly played during the radio broadcast announcing the arrival of {{ill|Douglas MacArthur|en|Douglas MacArthur|WD=Q127417|lt=Douglas MacArthur|1=Douglas MacArthur}} at the {{ill|Naval Air Facility Atsugi|en|Naval Air Facility Atsugi|WD=Q3016710|lt=Atsugi Naval Airfield|1=Naval Air Facility Atsugi}} on August 30, 1945, although it is unclear if it was performed live at the site.
Inspired by the name and legend of the now-forgotten "Kishimai," Ifukube composed the piece with his own interpretation. Structured in a {{ill|Rondo|en|Rondo|WD=Q13323|lt=rondo form|1=Rondo}}, its second theme, a march, was later adapted for various films, including the {{ill|Japan Self-Defense Forces|en|Japan Self-Defense Forces|WD=Q275488|lt=Self-Defense Forces|1=Japan Self-Defense Forces}}' mobilization scenes in {{ill|Godzilla (franchise)|en|Godzilla (franchise)|WD=Q28859760|lt=Godzilla (1954 film)|1=Godzilla (franchise)}}. This adaptation became the "Fregate March," used in themes such as the frigate fleet of the {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|en|Japan Coast Guard|WD=Q565418|lt=Japan Coast Guard|1=Japan Coast Guard}} in ''Godzilla'' and the "Space War March," the main theme for ''{{ill|Battle in Outer Space|en|Battle in Outer Space|WD=Q519893|lt=Battle in Outer Space|1=Battle in Outer Space}}''. Among these, the arrangement for ''{{ill|Invasion of Astro-Monster|en|Invasion of Astro-Monster|WD=Q814166|lt=Invasion of Astro-Monster|1=Invasion of Astro-Monster}}'' became widely known as the "Monster Zero March." This rendition was revisited in ''{{ill|Godzilla vs. Biollante|en|Godzilla vs. Biollante|WD=Q1514098|lt=Godzilla vs. Biollante|1=Godzilla vs. Biollante}}.''
Though forgotten after the war, "Kishimai" was "rediscovered" in the 1990s and was revived in May 2001. It is now occasionally performed by groups such as the {{ill|JGSDF Central Band|ja|陸上自衛隊中央音楽隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JGSDF Central Band|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 音楽隊 (陸上自衛隊)|1=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force Music Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664287}}.
